expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1: 'Identified site type: **portfolio**'. Enhancement report: 'Site type detected: **portfolio**'. metrics.json: '"site_type": "portfolio"'.

expectations.text: GSAP and ScrollTrigger scripts are added to the file
expectations.evidence: HTML output includes three CDN script tags: gsap@3.14/dist/gsap.min.js, gsap@3.14/dist/ScrollTrigger.min.js, gsap@3.14/dist/SplitText.min.js. Line 151: gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger, SplitText).

expectations.text: Phase A identifies issues: no scroll animations (CRITICAL), no reduced-motion guard (CRITICAL)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1 audit lists 'Zero scroll animations -- CRITICAL' and 'No gsap.matchMedia() -- CRITICAL'. Enhancement report Phase A confirms both were identified and fixed as CRITICAL items.

expectations.text: Phase B adds animations from the motion-dev pattern library
expectations.evidence: Enhancement report Phase B lists 15 new animations sourced from named patterns (Pattern 1, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 14). All are verifiable in the HTML output as GSAP code with pattern comments (e.g., 'Pattern 14 — Custom Cursor Follower', 'Pattern 4 — Project items stagger scroll reveal').

expectations.text: Hero heading gets kinetic typography (SplitText or similar)
expectations.evidence: HTML lines 174-196: new SplitText('.hero h1', { type: 'lines,words', mask: 'lines', autoSplit: true, aria: true }) with heroTl.fromTo(heroSplit.words, { yPercent: 110, autoAlpha: 0 }, { yPercent: 0, autoAlpha: 1, stagger: { each: 0.05, from: 'start' } }).

expectations.text: Project items get stagger scroll reveal animation
expectations.evidence: HTML lines 210-222: gsap.utils.toArray('.project-item').forEach with gsap.fromTo(item, { autoAlpha: 0, y: 40 }, { autoAlpha: 1, y: 0, delay: i * 0.09, scrollTrigger: { trigger: item, start: 'top 85%' } }). Each item gets its own ScrollTrigger with staggered delay.

expectations.text: About section gets scroll-triggered entrance animation
expectations.evidence: HTML lines 244-292: Four separate ScrollTrigger animations covering the about section: .about-label (line 245), .about-text (line 255), .skills-list li stagger reveal (line 267), and second .about-label (line 283). All use scrollTrigger with trigger and start properties.

expectations.text: gsap.matchMedia wraps all animation code with reduced-motion guard
expectations.evidence: HTML line 166: const mm = gsap.matchMedia(). Line 168: mm.add('(prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)', () => { ... }) wraps ALL animation code. Lines 379-391: mm.add('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)', () => { gsap.set([...], { clearProps: 'all' }); if (lenis) lenis.destroy(); }) provides the fallback branch.

expectations.text: Summary report follows the specified format with APPLIED/SKIPPED/N-A status per pattern
expectations.evidence: Enhancement report lines 34-51 list each considered pattern with explicit status: 6 APPLIED (with details), 5 SKIPPED (with reasons like 'no card containers', 'no horizontal panel content structure', 'status: declining'), 7 N/A (with reasons like 'no bento grid layout detected'). Legend at bottom defines APPLIED/SKIPPED/N/A/ALREADY_ANIMATED/PASSED.

expectations.text: No CSS transition conflicts with newly added GSAP animations
expectations.evidence: Transcript documents removal of conflicting CSS transitions: nav links (GSAP magnetic pull owns x/y), skills-list li (GSAP scroll reveal owns autoAlpha/x), footer links (GSAP magnetic pull owns x/y). Two remaining CSS transitions are safe: .project-title transition: color (GSAP animates x/y/autoAlpha on parent .project-item, not color on child), .contact-email transition: color/border-bottom-color (GSAP animates autoAlpha/y, not color). HTML includes comments documenting each removal decision.

claims.evidence: Transcript audit lists 4 CRITICAL items (zero scroll animations, only opacity animated, no kinetic typography, no gsap.matchMedia). Enhancement report confirms all 4 fixed. HTML output contains scroll animations, transform-based animations, SplitText kinetic typography, and gsap.matchMedia wrapper.

claims.claim: 2 WARNING issues found and fixed (CSS transition conflicts)
claims.evidence: Transcript lists CSS transition conflicts on nav links and footer links. HTML shows removal comments and no conflicting transitions remain on GSAP-owned properties.

Enhancement report lists 15 individually enumerated animations in Phase B. HTML output contains corresponding GSAP code blocks for each: hero SplitText, hero subtitle, projects header, 6 project items scroll reveal, 6 project items spring hover, 2 about labels, about text, 8 skills list items, contact label, contact email, footer, custom cursor, magnetic pull, Lenis. Count checks out.

Counting animated selectors: hero h1 (1), subtitle (1), projects-header (1), project-items (6 scroll + 6 hover = 6 unique), about-labels (2), about-text (1), skills-list li (8), contact-label (1), contact-email (1), footer (1), cursor dot/ring (2), nav links (3 magnetic), footer social (4 magnetic) = 33 elements.

Transcript Step 3.5 and 3.6 list syntax and conflict checks all passing. HTML output appears syntactically well-formed: all braces/parentheses balanced, no placeholder text, registerPlugin present, matchMedia guard present.

Searched HTML script section: all animation properties use autoAlpha, not bare opacity. The only 'opacity' reference in the GSAP code is for the cursor ring (autoAlpha: 0.8 / 0.5), which correctly uses autoAlpha.
HTML lines 371-375: cleanup return function calls heroSplit.revert() and heroTl.kill().
HTML line 390: if (lenis) lenis.destroy(); inside the (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) matchMedia branch.

suggestions.assertion: GSAP and ScrollTrigger scripts are added to the file
suggestions.reason: This checks presence but not version pinning or CDN choice. The skill actually pinned to gsap@3.14 and used a specific Lenis version. Consider asserting pinned versions to catch future drift.

suggestions.assertion: Hero heading gets kinetic typography (SplitText or similar)
suggestions.reason: The 'or similar' clause is appropriately flexible, but consider also checking that SplitText cleanup (revert) is present in a cleanup function, since SplitText without cleanup causes memory leaks and accessibility issues on re-renders.
No assertion checks for Lenis smooth scroll integration, which was added and is a significant portfolio-specific enhancement. The skill applied it as a high-scoring portfolio pattern.

No assertion checks for custom cursor follower, which is a substantial interactive feature added to the page (cursor dot + ring with mix-blend-mode, hover scaling). It has CSS fallbacks and touch guards worth verifying.
No assertion verifies that magnetic cursor pull was added to nav/footer links, another interactive enhancement specific to portfolio sites.

No assertion checks for touch-device guards (pointer: coarse media queries, isTouch checks). The skill correctly gates cursor and magnetic effects behind fine-pointer detection, which is an important quality signal.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the core enhancement workflow well (detect, audit, fix, enrich, guard, report). However, several significant interactive features added by the skill (custom cursor, magnetic pull, Lenis smooth scroll) go unasserted. Adding assertions for these would better test the skill's portfolio-specific enrichment capability and differentiate a thorough run from a minimal one.
